metadata.description: Rulebook for jurisdictional conflict detection between Federal and Provincial laws in Pakistan.
metadata.source: Constitution of Pakistan (relevant articles + Fourth Schedule)

fundamental_rights.summary: Guarantees basic rights including privacy, dignity, free speech, information access, movement, equality.

distribution_of_powers.summary: Defines boundaries of Federal vs Provincial legislative authority and conflict resolution rules.

privacy_related.summary: Article 14: privacy of home guaranteed; Article 19/19A: freedom of speech and access to information.

federal_supremacy.rule: If a provincial law conflicts with a federal law in a subject on the Federal Legislative List, the federal law prevails.

provincial_autonomy.rule: Provinces have exclusive right to legislate on subjects not listed in the Federal Legislative List.

joint_authority.rule: Where a subject touches both federal and provincial matters (e.g., health + ICT jurisdiction), a review is required to determine overlap.

federal_legislative_list.summary: Exclusive authority of the Federal Government.

provincial_list.summary: Matters under exclusive provincial control after the 18th amendment.

ict_special_case.summary: Islamabad Capital Territory (ICT) falls under Federal domain.
ict_special_case.implication: Policies involving CDA, ICT Building Codes, or Islamabad regulations must align with Federal law.

National Cyber Security Policy 2021.reason: Cybersecurity is a Federal Legislative subject (national security + ICT).

Punjab Primary and Secondary Healthcare.reason: Healthcare falls under Provincial List after 18th amendment.

CDA - Ground Rent and Land Use (ICT) Ame.reason: ICT region + CDA are under Federal control.

Personal Data Protection Bill 2023.reason: Telecom + digital privacy fall under Federal List and PTA jurisdiction.
